networks.name: Yazing
networks.notes: Secondary platform; routes commission through Yazing's own structure. Positioned as 100% commission pass-through to affiliates.

commission.notes: 5% on all sales. No tiered or performance-bonus structure detected. Unchanged across all 2026 audit cycles.

affiliate_program.shipping: Same business day on orders before 12:00 noon ET

creative_assets.notes: No self-serve public creative library. Priority team available to assist with additional marketing content needs. AvantLink dashboard provides standard banners, product feeds, and deep links to approved affiliates.

affiliate_program.approval_requirements: Active website or social channel with original outdoor/recreation content; coupon/rebate/deal-aggregator sites not accepted; manual review via AvantLink

product_catalog.key_differentiator: Low-maintenance Gates Carbon belt drive (rust-free, grease-free) across all models
product_catalog.flagship_ebike: Priority Current Plus E-Bike (750W mid-drive motor, electronic shifting, 600% gear range, ~$3,299)

vs_benchmark.commission: at_median — 5% matches category median; Velowave (7-10%) and Velotric (7%) remain above
vs_benchmark.cookie: below_median — 21 days vs 30-day category median; 4th consecutive audit cycle with no change
vs_benchmark.aov: below_median (standard line at $550); above_median on e-bike line (~$3,299 Current Plus = ~$165 EPC)

afare_signals.commission_note: At category median (5%). No improvement trend. E-bike competitors Velowave (7-10%) and Velotric (7%) remain structurally more lucrative for e-bike-focused affiliate creators.
afare_signals.cookie_note: 21-day cookie — 4th consecutive Priority Bicycles audit with no change. Below 30-day category median. High-consideration bicycle purchases regularly span multiple sessions; attribution gap is a real deterrent for sophisticated affiliates.
afare_signals.aov_note: Standard bike AOV ~$550 → ~$27.50 EPC at 5%. E-bike Current Plus ~$3,299 → ~$165 EPC. E-bike content remains ~6× more valuable per conversion.
strengths: Unique belt-drive low-maintenance positioning — strong, repeatable content hook for lifestyle, commuter, and parent creators
Same-day shipping + 7-day customer support = conversion-positive proof points for affiliate content
30-day return policy reduces buyer hesitation — easy confident recommendation
Affirm financing expands addressable buyer pool on higher-ticket e-bikes
AvantLink is a trusted, fraud-resistant network with deep-link and product-feed tooling
Costco Next listing signals mainstream brand credibility
Yazing secondary platform adds affiliate discovery surface
weaknesses: 21-day cookie stagnant — now 4 consecutive audit cycles below the 30-day category standard with no improvement signal
5% flat commission — no tiered, performance-bonus, or seasonal rate structure ever detected
No self-serve creative library; custom asset requests require direct Priority team outreach
Single primary network (AvantLink) limits organic affiliate discovery
E-bike high-AOV/EPC opportunity under-promoted in affiliate materials
opportunities: PEAK SEASON: April 19 — prime spring buying window; affiliate content live now captures peak April/May demand
E-bike EPC: Current Plus at $3,299 × 5% = ~$165 EPC — among the highest in DTC bike affiliate space
Travel/lifestyle content angle via hotel brand partnerships (Starwood, Nantucket) for travel creators
Urban car-free lifestyle content — belt-drive narrative resonates with commuter and sustainability audiences
Kids bike segment — rust/grease-free belt drive is unique safety and zero-maintenance pitch for parent channels
Comparison content (Priority vs. Rad Power, Aventon) performs well organically
threats: Velowave (7-10% / 60-day cookie) and Velotric (7% / 30-day) offer materially better affiliate economics in e-bike segment
Aventon (4% / 30-day) and Rad Power Bikes (5% / 30-day) match or beat Priority cookie window at similar rates
Costco Next listing creates price-comparison bypass risk for affiliate links
Program stagnation across 4 audit cycles signals low affiliate channel investment — risk of creator attention shifting to higher-paying programs
No spring 2026 promotional rate or creative campaign detected — missed seasonal amplification opportunity

changes_since_last_audit.change_summary: No changes detected. Commission (5%), cookie (21 days), networks (AvantLink primary / Yazing secondary), AOV ($550+), and all program terms are fully unchanged from 2026-04-17 audit. Program has been static with no modifications since at least mid-2024.
changes_since_last_audit.program_stability: high — zero rate changes, network additions, creative library launches, or structural modifications observed across all 2026 audit cycles

benchmark_update.notes: bicycles_dtc benchmarks remain accurate. uppromote.com February 2026 data and Priority's own program page confirm 5%/21-day terms consistent with current benchmark values. No update to category-benchmarks.json required.

recommended_actions: PEAK SEASON NOW: April 19 is prime spring window — LiveChannel vendors should have affiliate content live immediately to capture April/May demand peak
E-bike focus: Current Plus ~$3,299 at 5% = ~$165 EPC — coach creators to lead e-bike content, not standard bikes
Escalate cookie concern formally: 4 consecutive audits at 21 days against 30-day category standard — time to make a direct ask to Priority affiliate contact for cookie extension
Flag to LiveChannel: Velowave (7-10%/60-day) and Velotric (7%/30-day) now offer structurally better affiliate economics in e-bike segment — consider rebalancing creator partner recommendations
Monitor AvantLink program dashboard and newsletter for any spring 2026 promotional rate, bonus period, or creative asset drop — none detected to date
